title: Direct Routing and Bounded Worker Pools · Language Lens: Default Exchange Coupling
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must combine direct routing with bounded goroutines, prefetch, dependency capacity, and acknowledgement after durable effect; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the default exchange coupling increment in a capacity-aware Go work distribution service. Learners pr…
objectives: Explain the default exchange queue-name binding and choose it only when direct queue-name coupling is intentional; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Migrate incompatible topology with versioned names, dual bindings, drain evidence, producer and consumer ordering, and rollback; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Default Exchange Coupling · Theory Default Exchange Coupling
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go messaging team handles case 3617 during outbox relay recovery after a database commit. Build deterministic Go evidence to combine direct routing, worker capacity, and acknowledgement-after-effect evidence; retain rollback topology before deleting old bindings; then add a subscriber responsibility without changing publisher intent. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Explain the default exchange queue-name binding and choose it only when direct queue-name coupling is intentional; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
